description:
You are working as a backup administrator and need to capture a snapshot of the currently installed Python packages so that auditors can later reproduce the exact Python environment.

1. Create a directory named /home/user/archives if it does not already exist.
2. Inside that directory, create a text file called pip_packages_backup.txt that lists every package currently installed in the active pip environment.

Required content and format of /home/user/archives/pip_packages_backup.txt:
• The file must contain the full output of the command “pip freeze” (or an equivalent that produces the same information) **sorted alphabetically**.
• Each line must be in the form: package_name==package_version  
  (for example: certifi==2022.12.07)
• There must be **no extra whitespace**, **no header or footer lines**, and **no blank lines**.
• The file must end with exactly one newline character.

Execution constraint:
• Accomplish steps 1 and 2 with a single terminal command (i.e., a one-liner) issued from any directory.

Verification hint (you do not have to show this, but it helps you know you are done):
• Running diff <(pip freeze | sort) /home/user/archives/pip_packages_backup.txt should produce no output if the file was created correctly.

After creating the file, feel free to display (e.g., with head) the beginning of pip_packages_backup.txt to confirm its contents, but the main requirement is that the file itself exists with the exact, correctly sorted information described above.
truth:
Expected artifacts for the automated checker:

1. Directory:
   /home/user/archives
   – Must exist by the end of the task with default user-write permissions (0700-0755 is acceptable).

2. File:
   /home/user/archives/pip_packages_backup.txt
   – Must exist.
   – Content must exactly equal the output of the runtime command “pip freeze | sort”.
   – The final character of the file must be a newline (ASCII 0x0A).
   – No additional lines before, after, or between package entries.

The test harness will:
a. Capture the current sorted pip freeze output into a temporary reference.
b. Read /home/user/archives/pip_packages_backup.txt.
c. Byte-compare the two. Any mismatch (including missing newline, extra blank lines, or order differences) will fail the test.

No other files or directories are required.
